title: Count Good Nodes in Binary Tree
problem_statement: Count the number of nodes in a binary tree where each node’s value is at least the maximum value along the path from the root to that node.

examples.explanation: Good nodes are 3 (root), 4, 5, and 3 on the left branch.

constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [1, 10^5]
intuition: Visit each node while carrying the maximum value seen so far from the root and count nodes that meet or exceed that maximum.

approaches.name: Preorder traversal with path maximum
approaches.description: Use recursion to propagate the max value on the path and count the current node when it is good.
approaches.why_not: A bottom-up traversal would require more complicated state merging and is unnecessary here.
interview_commentary: Track the path maximum from root to each node.
Count nodes when their value meets or exceeds that maximum.
Update maxSoFar before exploring children if the current node is good.

interview_qa.q: What information is carried to child nodes?
interview_qa.a: The maximum value seen so far along the path from the root.

interview_qa.q: How do you decide if a node is good?
interview_qa.a: If its value is greater than or equal to the path maximum.

interview_qa.q: Why is this an O(n) solution?
interview_qa.a: Each node is visited exactly once.

interview_qa.q: What happens when the current node is good?
interview_qa.a: Update the path maximum before processing its children.
